So that's what Google accounts as an engaged session.
And it's actually important for SEO because Google, like if a web page has a very high bounce rate, which is the opposite.

We sometimes use the query report to do topic research.

Sometimes we might identify that you're ranking on page two or three for a keyword that would be really valuable to your business, but the page that you're ranking for the keyword is not optimized for that keyword at all.
So what we can do is create a blog post that could actually rank on page one because you're already ranking so highly and you don't even have content around that keyword.
An example would be the first client that I had when I joined GrowthX.
They're a dental staffing platform, and we discovered that they were ranking for the keyword TRO reviews, even though they didn't have any content on their website that listed different user reviews.

user reviews from different platforms, and we kind of created a nice narrative and, like, packaged the reviews into different sections that talked about different benefits of using their platform, and that blog post ranked pretty much immediately in the first position for that keyword, and to this day, they get a lot of leads precisely from that blog post because it's very, very bottom of the funnel content that doesn't get a lot of search volume, but it is valuable for, you know, the bottom line.
